trajectory.description: Observation: Runtime error 'TypeError: sum() received an invalid combination of arguments' in calculate_laplacian because adj_mx was converted to torch tensor before being passed to np.sum. Analysis: In STGCN init, we convert numpy adj_mx to torch, then call calculate_laplacian(adj_mx) which expects numpy. Need to fix order: compute laplacian on numpy first, then convert to torch. Decision: Fix model.py, rerun training for 5 epochs, then submit eval. Also add a fallback in predict.py to recalculate A_hat from adj_mx if not in checkpoint (safer).

trajectory.description: Observation: predict.py fails because torch.load default weights_only=True can't handle numpy arrays saved in checkpoint (train_mean, train_std). Analysis: Our checkpoint includes numpy arrays alongside model state_dict, which triggers the error. Fix: add weights_only=False to torch.load in predict.py. Also need to ensure model loads correctly. Decision: fix predict.py, retrain with slight capacity increase to improve baseline, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline mae_60min = 5.0671 with tiny STGCN (hidden 32, blocks 2). Training 10 epochs took 89s. Analysis: Model is very small, no activation after GCN, no residuals, no dropout. Increasing capacity and adding these components should improve performance. Remaining time ~2.95h allows longer training and larger models. Decision: Scale up model (hidden=64, spatial=32, blocks=3), add ReLU after GCN, add residual connections, add dropout 0.1, increase batch_size to 64, train 15 epochs, submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: eval failed because predict.py got 'STGCN.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: adj_mx'. Analysis: training code (train.py) saves model_kwargs without 'adj_mx', so when predict.py loads the saved model_kwargs (which exist but lack adj_mx), it fails. Decision: fix predict.py to ensure adj_mx is added to model_kwargs if missing, and also update train.py to save adj_mx in model_kwargs for future. Then resubmit eval for best.pt (from iter 6) to get the real mae_60min score.

trajectory.description: Observation: mae_60min improved to 3.9956 with hidden=128, spatial=64, blocks=4, 40 epochs. Model still learning (val_mae declining). Analysis: To push lower, need larger model, longer training, weighted loss focusing on far horizon, cosine annealing for better convergence, gradient clipping for stability, and an optional MLP decoder to replace the simple linear head. Remaining time ~2.7h allows ~20-30 min runs. Decision: Increase model to hidden=160, spatial=80, blocks=5, add MLP decoder, weighted L1 loss with linear weight increase (alpha=0.2), cosine annealing with warm restarts (T_0=30, T_mult=2), gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0), train for 100 epochs, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: mae_60min improved to 3.8742 with current STGCN (hidden=160, blocks=5, MLP decoder, weighted loss). Val loss plateau ~0.321. Analysis: Need more expressive spatial modeling (adaptive graph structure) and better temporal receptive field (dilated convolutions) to capture non-local dependencies. Decision: Implement AdaptiveSTGCN with learnable adjacency, dilated temporal convolutions, exponential weighting, cosine schedule w/o restarts, and increased capacity (hidden=192, spatial=96). Train 80 epochs (~40min). Submit eval.
